embeds.title: 🕯️ incident report — the night the horde didn't ride
embeds.description:
**Some of you noticed the drains went quiet last night.** From Monday midnight to Tuesday dawn, not one ride went out. Six hours. Your hordes stood saddled at the gate in the dark. Nobody blew the horn.

**what happened**
An internal investigation (it took eleven minutes, he confessed immediately, then asked if he was getting overtime for the interview) has identified the culprit: **Maurice "Two-Naps" Squeakins**, third-shift horn-blower, employee of the Drain Authority for six weeks and asleep for roughly five of them. At the stroke of midnight Maurice consulted the calendar, concluded that Monday "didn't feel finished," hung the **recruitment-window sign** on the gate — a sign that comes out ONE morning a week, which Maurice knows, because it is his ONLY other duty — and bedded down in the scrap barrow. The dawn shift found him at 06:00. The horn was found later. Unblown. Used as a pillow.

**what it cost you**
• six hourly hauls, skipped for **everyone equally** — nobody gained an edge, the leaderboard is untouched
• those hours are gone. the Drain Authority does not back-pay scrap, and neither does Maurice, who is compensated in cheese rinds and has already eaten them

**what happens now**
• the gate schedule has been rewritten so the sign **physically cannot be hung** on any night but the season's first morning — that fix rides out with Monday's reset, before the night shift gets another chance
• Maurice has been reassigned to counting the felled. he counts out loud. he is bad at it
• his performance review has been nailed to the gate as a warning to others

we thank you for your patience, and Maurice would too, if he were awake. 🐀

footer.text: we ride at dawn · the horn blows hourly · allegedly
